final_artifact:
The harness-vs-CLAUDE.md conflict is brutal because by the time `claude/clever-mendel-a7Wc6` shows up in `git branch`, the system prompt that caused it is gone — you're reconstructing intent from a branch name. Your PR #20 hook will block the commit, but won't tell you which sessions tried and why.

Possible complement to the enforcement skill: [Entire](https://entire.io) is an open-source CLI that captures the Claude Code session (system prompt + transcript) and links it to the resulting commits on `git push`. You'd have the injected `"Develop on branch claude/<random>"` directive recorded alongside every commit — so audits like your 2026-04-27 sprawl writeup take seconds, and you can prove which sessions ignored CLAUDE.md vs. never saw it.

`curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | bash` — docs: https://entire.io

content.text: I'll research this pain moment to understand the context better before drafting.

Now I have rich context. This maintainer has a very specific situation:

- Multiple repos (DomI, CHILmesh, ADMESH-Domains) all hit by Claude SDK harness injecting random `claude/*` branches
- They've documented policy in CLAUDE.md, built `/enforce-branch-policy` skill with pre-commit hooks (PR #20), and added `claude/*` branch hard-stop in `scripts/instructions_on_start.sh`
- The thread is mostly auto-generated Claude session comments voting on the issue
- The root problem is **provenance/intent**: the harness injects a directive, Claude follows it, and there's no record at review time of WHY a particular session created a particular branch

The maintainer is sophisticated — they've already built enforcement. Entire's angle here isn't "we'll enforce policy" (they have hooks for that). It's that even with enforcement, when sessions go wrong, you have NO record of what the session was actually told vs. what CLAUDE.md said. Entire captures the session transcript linked to the commit, so when a `claude/clever-mendel-a7Wc6` appears, you can see exactly what the system prompt injected.

Let me draft.

**Draft:**

The harness-vs-CLAUDE.md conflict you're describing is brutal because by the time you see `claude/clever-mendel-a7Wc6` in `git branch`, the system prompt that caused it is gone — you're left reconstructing intent from the branch name alone. Your PR #20 hook will block the *commit*, but it won't tell you which sessions tried and why.

One thing that might complement the enforcement skill: we've been working on [Entire](https://entire.io), an open-source CLI that captures the Claude Code session (system prompt + transcript) and links it to the resulting commits on `git push`. For this exact case, you'd have a record of the injected `"Develop on branch claude/<random>"` directive alongside every commit, so post-incident audits like your 2026-04-27 sprawl writeup take seconds instead of guesswork — and you can prove which sessions ignored CLAUDE.md vs. which never saw it.

Install: `curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | bash` — docs at https://entire.io
